Home»Sport»Paris Olympics 2024»Paris 2024: Rebecca Meder sets her goals in the pool

Star swimmer Rebecca Meder is hoping to make it to the semifinals of her second Olympic Games in Paris.

Rebecca Meder is hoping to make it to the semifinals of her secondOlympic Gamesin Paris.

The 21-year-old has a week to prepare before participating in the women’s 200-meter individual medley heats on 2 August  in the morning session, even though the swimming competitions officially begin today.

She’ll swim again that evening at 9:22 pm, if she makes it to the semifinals. Despite her aspirations, Meder wants to take things one step at a time and avoid placing too much pressure on herself.

“Swimming starts first but I’m towards the end of the swimming programme, so I’ve got a week until I race which is pretty exciting. My goals would be obviously to be the best that I can be, I know that’s a cliche answer, everyone says that but I train extremely hard,” Meder toldSABC Sports.

“It’s my second Olympic Games, I’m still young, I’m 21 years old but I still have a lot to achieve in my swimming career. I’m chasing that semifinal and once that semifinal is in, then I’m hoping to try and make that final.

“It’s a big and scary dream but whether I get it or I don’t, I’m going to fight my way to try and see what I can do not just to make my family proud but my country proud and my God proud. So, that’s my goal, that’s my dream.”

Despite being in Paris for her second Olympic Games, she feels as though everything is brand-new.

She can’t believe she’s going to another Olympics and is excited to meet Gayton McKenzie, the new sports minister.

“Ooh, not yet I got up, hopped on the bus and I’m now here,” she said when asked about meeting the Minister.

“I think it’s gonna be awesome meeting him and introducing myself and getting to know him too but even this whole Olympic Games experience you often have to stop and pinch yourself and remind yourself that you are here,” she added.

“I saw the competition pool for the first time yesterday and normally everyone walks in and they are like, wow and take videos and whatever and I actually just stopped and I just let it sink in and then tears were in my eyes a little bit and I was like, this is the moment that you’ve trained so hard for.

“Yes, I’ve been to the Olympic Games but that doesn’t make this one’s not as special. Every single time I get to represent my country, every time I get to see the rings, it’s very special.”

title: Paris 2024: Rebecca Meder sets her goals in the pool
author: James Richardson
description: Star swimmer Rebecca Meder is hoping to make it to the semifinals of her second Olympic Games in Paris.
